subject: UX & Research
title: Wikipedia's Edit Button Research
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Wikipedia's Edit Button Research.

hook.text: Wikipedia struggled for years to get new editors. Research showed the edit button was invisible to most readers.

buildup.text: The 'Edit' tab looked like navigation, not an action. Users didn't realize anyone could modify articles.

discovery.text: A/B testing a more prominent edit button increased edits by 10%. Simply making it visible changed participation.

twist.text: But more clicks also meant more vandalism. Every interface change has second-order effects you must measure.

climax.text: Wikipedia now combines a visible edit button with better moderation tools. Encourage participation and manage quality.

punchline.text: Visible affordances drive action. But plan for the consequences too.

quiz.question: What happened when Wikipedia made the edit button more prominent?
options: Edits increased 10%, but so did vandalism — requiring better moderation
Vandalism decreased because more good editors participated
Nothing changed because users already knew they could edit
